name: 5 Star Car Title Loans
description_short: 5 Star Car Title Loans offers vehicle-secured loans from $100–$50,000 with same-day funding across ~18 states. Borrowers keep their car throughout the loan.
description_long:
5 Star Car Title Loans is a consumer lending company headquartered in San Bernardino, California, that has been operating since 2012. Over its 14-year history the company reports having funded more than 255,000 loans across multiple states. It is a licensed and regulated lender that operates both online and through physical branch locations, currently serving customers in approximately 18 states including California, Florida, Texas, Ohio, Michigan, Virginia, and others.

5 Star Car Title Loans specializes in loans secured by a vehicle's title. Its primary product is the car title loan, where the borrower pledges their vehicle title as collateral while retaining the vehicle for personal use throughout the repayment period. Loan amounts range from $100 to $50,000, with the upper end determined by the vehicle's assessed value. The company also offers installment loans with fixed monthly payments and repayment terms of up to 24 months, as well as same-day loans for urgent needs. Beyond standard passenger vehicles, the company accepts motorcycles and semi-trucks as collateral based on customer testimonials on its website.

The company emphasizes speed and accessibility as core differentiators. Approved applicants can receive funds the same day they apply, and the process is available both online and in person. 5 Star explicitly markets to borrowers with imperfect credit, positioning vehicle equity rather than credit score as the primary qualification factor. The company advertises no prepayment penalties and claims transparent, upfront terms with no hidden fees. It holds a 4.7-star rating based on 11 Google reviews at the time of this profile.

As with all title loans, prospective borrowers should be aware of the material risks. The company's website does not disclose annual percentage rates (APR) or specific fee schedules, which makes cost comparison difficult before applying. Title loans as a product class typically carry very high APRs, and failure to repay puts the borrower's vehicle at risk of repossession. The 11-review sample is a thin basis for evaluating quality at the scale of 255,000+ funded loans. 5 Star is a legitimate licensed lender suitable for borrowers who have exhausted lower-cost alternatives and own a vehicle with sufficient equity.
address: 801 W St Rd 436 Suite 2179, Altamonte Springs, FL 32714

company_info.headquarters: 801 W St Rd 436 Suite 2179, Altamonte Springs, FL 32714

pros: Operating since 2012 with 255,000+ loans funded — established track record
Borrower keeps and drives the vehicle throughout the loan term
Same-day funding available for approved applicants
Loan amounts up to $50,000 based on vehicle equity
No prepayment penalties — pay off early without additional cost
Licensed and regulated lender with advertised transparent terms and no hidden fees
Accepts motorcycles and semi-trucks as collateral, not only passenger vehicles
cons: No APR or interest rate information displayed on the website — costs unknown before applying
Title loan default risk: vehicle can be repossessed if the loan is not repaid
Only 11 published reviews despite claiming 255,000+ loans funded — very thin public feedback
Geographic coverage limited to approximately 18 states — unavailable in many US states
No mention of credit counseling, hardship programs, or alternatives for struggling borrowers
best_for: Vehicle owners with sufficient equity who need fast cash and have poor or no credit
Borrowers facing same-day financial emergencies (rent, bills, medical) who own a paid-off or low-balance vehicle
People needing larger emergency amounts ($5,000–$50,000) who cannot qualify for unsecured personal loans
Residents of one of the ~18 states where 5 Star has licensed locations
diagnosis: 5 Star Car Title Loans is best for vehicle owners with poor credit who need fast cash and have no lower-cost borrowing options available. The main caveat is that APR and fee details are not disclosed on the website, and title loans as a product class carry high interest costs and the risk of losing your vehicle if you default.
services: Car title loans (secured by vehicle title, borrower keeps car)
Installment loans (fixed payments, up to 24-month terms)
